title: Xtra Fluffy Filipino Pork Blood Dinuguan
description: A fatty and fluffy Filipino dish featuring pork blood, vegetable shortening, and shio-cure, perfect for a flexitarian diet. This recipe is cooked in a cast-iron skillet to achieve a crispy exterior and a tender interior.
instructions: Heat the vegetable shortening in a cast-iron skillet over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
Add the pork blood, shio-cure, vinegar, salt, black pepper, and bay leaves to the skillet.
Stir well to combine and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce the heat to low and cook, stirring occasionally, for 20 minutes or until the dinuguan has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
Remove the bay leaves and serve the dinuguan hot, garnished with chopped green onions if desired.
